message:
feat(runtime): Implement vtable-based dynamic dispatch for PyValue.object

Implements a vtable pattern for PyValue.object instances to enable dynamic
method dispatch while preserving type erasure. This solves the fundamental
problem of calling dunder methods on class instances wrapped in PyValue.

Changes:
- Added PyObjectVTable struct with function pointers for rich comparison methods
  (__eq__, __ne__, __lt__, __le__, __gt__, __ge__)
- Implemented generateVTable() to create vtables at comptime using @hasDecl
- Updated ObjectInstance to include vtable pointer alongside type-erased ptr
- Modified fromAlloc() to detect class instances and generate vtables
- Updated callDunderMethod() to use vtable for dynamic dispatch instead of stub

Pattern follows async/future/waker.zig WakerVTable design:
- Comptime reflection generates wrapper functions per type
- Wrappers cast *anyopaque back to concrete type and call methods
- Single vtable instance per type (stored in struct-local const)

Testing:
- test_eq.py confirms __eq__ is called correctly through vtable
- Rich comparison protocol with NotImplemented handling works as expected

Related:
- test_binop still fails due to pre-existing callable PyValue invocation issue
  (self.logger(args) where logger is PyValue - unrelated to vtable)
